summary: Iteration 1 - Backend fully passing (15/15 pytest). Frontend login/logo/protected-routes/sidebar/employee CRUD/logout/settings page verified. Blocker: Cannot reach AssetDetail form (new asset creation) via /assets/new route or 'New Asset' button; URL remains /assets and field-category never renders.

ui_bugs.issue: Navigation to /assets/new (direct URL AND via 'New Asset' button which calls navigate('/assets/new')) does not render the AssetDetail form. URL stays at /assets and field-category / section-basic never appear. Two 404 console errors observed during attempt (likely /api/assets/new being fetched because isNew check may not work on re-render). This blocks: asset creation, tech-specs conditional, department autofill, lock/edit/save cycle, asset_id auto-gen UI test, delete.

issues: Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' (a11y)

action_items: Investigate /assets/new routing - verify <Route path='/assets/new'> precedes <Route path='/assets/:id'> match (it does in App.js) and confirm AssetDetail.jsx does not prematurely navigate away when id==='new'. The 2 console 404 errors suggest GET /api/assets/new is being fired despite isNew early-return in loadAsset(). Check that useEffect deps [id] and the early-return are correct in production build.

Once /assets/new renders, re-test: (a) tech-specs visibility for Mobile vs Laptop, (b) department auto-fill when selecting employee, (c) save → locked-mode redirect to /assets/:id with auto-generated LAP- prefix, (d) edit cycle, (e) delete, (f) maintenance add/delete on detail page, (g) Settings change-password toasts.
Add DialogDescription to AlertDialog instances to fix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: user['department'] leakage in employees CRUD response is fine. DELETE /employees does NOT require 204; returns 200 which is acceptable.
server.py Pydantic AssetIn uses Literal for status but update_asset accepts AssetIn which also validates purchase_price type - ensure empty string passes (frontend sends null already).

AssetDetail.jsx: useEffect dependency on [id] is correct but after save on isNew, navigate(`/assets/${data.id}`) is called with new id - but component is already mounted so useEffect will re-trigger loadAsset. This is correct behaviour, just noting.
Login sets SameSite=None;Secure which requires HTTPS (works in preview). CORS allows '*' with credentials=True which browsers actually reject; consider explicit origin list if cookies fail in future.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook suggests 5-fail lockout) — not a current bug but a hardening item.
test_credentials: Admin/nothing, Admin2/nothing2 (both work on backend, Admin verified on frontend login)
seed_data_creation: Test employee 'TESTE2E_<ts>' with department 'DeptAutoFillE2E' created via UI and left in DB for retest. Backend test suite creates/cleans TEST_-prefixed employees/locations/assets/maintenance.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest). Reproduction for the AssetDetail blocker: login → click 'New Asset' button on /assets page OR navigate directly to /assets/new → URL stays at /assets and AssetDetail form never renders (no [data-testid='field-category']). 2 GET 404 errors appear in console. If main agent verifies the route + loadAsset early-return logic, then run the frontend E2E to test: (1) Mobile category hides [data-testid='section-tech'] while Laptop shows it, (2) selecting employee auto-fills [data-testid='field-department'] to employee's department, (3) save without asset_id auto-generates LAP-XXXX (h1 text), (4) saved asset redirects to /assets/:id in locked mode ([data-testid='edit-btn'] visible, no inputs), (5) Edit→change→Save cycles back to locked, (6) Delete via [data-testid='delete-confirm'] navigates to /assets, (7) /settings cp-submit with wrong cp-current shows 'Current password is incorrect' toast, mismatched cp-new vs cp-confirm shows mismatch error toast.
rca of the issue: Frontend /assets/new routing failure: Likely caused by either (a) React Router matching /assets prefix before /assets/new due to build-time concerns, or (b) AssetDetail.jsx loadAsset() not respecting isNew on first render (despite early return) causing a 404 then navigate('/assets') from the catch block. Evidence: 2× '404' console errors consistently appear, and URL ends at /assets (exactly what the catch block does: navigate('/assets')). Mitigation: In AssetDetail.jsx, ensure `if (isNew) { setLoading(false); return; }` at top of loadAsset (currently only `return`), and/or move the early-return OUTSIDE the try/catch so no navigation is triggered.
